data.description:
**Be a part of something BIG!**
Say
**HELLO to BIG Possibilities with Singtel KL**
(Sudong MY Sdn Bhd)!
  
  
Singtel is Asia's leading communications technology group, our presence spans across 21 countries in Asia, Australia, and Africa. We thrive on innovation, digital transformation, and harnessing next-generation technology to create a more sustainable and digital future. Now, we are bringing this energy to Kuala Lumpur with the launch of our brand-new Shared Services Centre.
  
  
The
**Billing Team Lead**
is responsible to
**oversee the**
**billing team operations**
by delivering accurate, compliant, and customer-focused billing outcomes for our
**customers**
. You'll manage a team of 5-15 specialists while driving task completion, quality excellence, and process efficiency. This position will play a critical part in ensuring our high-volume billing operations run seamlessly while supporting continuous improvement initiatives. Employees will be required to work according to the operational hours of the overseas operations they support.
  
  
**Make An Impact By**

- Lead & develop your team – Coach, mentor, and manage performance of billing operations specialists
- Ensure billing accuracy – Oversee provisioning, production, contract validation.
- Drive timely delivery – Manage workflows to meet SLAs, billing cycle deadlines, and operational cut-offs
- Resolve customer issues – Handle escalations, reduce disputes, and improve customer satisfaction
- Improve processes – Identify and implement efficiency improvements and defect reduction initiatives
- Maintain compliance – Ensure adherence to regulatory, audit, and internal policy requirements
- Collaborate across teams – Work closely with Sales, Finance, Product, Credit & Collections, and IT teams
- Provide insights – Deliver operational reporting and analytics to support decision-making

**Skills For Success**

- Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Commerce, or a related field, or a professional accounting qualification
- 5–8 years of experience in billing operations, revenue operations, or similar high‑volume transactional environments
- 2–3 years of experience in leading a team or operating as a senior subject matter expert within a Finance, Billing, or Customer Service function
- Comprehensive understanding of end‑to‑end billing processes across billing platforms such as Salesforce, SAP, Oracle, Amdocs, or equivalent systems
- Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (e.g. VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables) with solid working knowledge of MS Office applications
- Excellent analytical and problem‑solving capabilities, with a high level of accuracy and attention to detail
- Highly effective communication and stakeholder management skills, enabling clear and confident engagement across multiple levels
- Demonstrated ability in time management and prioritization, with experience handling multiple monthly deadlines and resolving issues efficiently
- An adaptable and collaborative team player who performs well in dynamic environments and maintains a proactive, solution‑oriented mindset

**Good to Have**

- Telecommunications, utilities, or subscription-based billing experience
- Six Sigma, Lean, or process improvement certification
- Experience supporting system migrations or transformation initiatives

company_object.name: Singtel
company_object.long_description:
Singtel is Asia's leading communications technology group, providing a portfolio of services from next-generation communication, 5G and technology services to infotainment to both consumers and businesses. The Group has presence in Asia, Australia and Africa and reaches over 740 million mobile customers in 21 countries. Its infrastructure and technology services for businesses span 21 countries, with more than 428 direct points of presence in 362 cities.  

For consumers, Singtel delivers a complete and integrated suite of services, including mobile, broadband and TV. For businesses, Singtel offers a complementary array of workforce mobility solutions, data hosting, cloud, network infrastructure, analytics and cyber security capabilities. 

Singtel is dedicated to continuous innovation, harnessing next-generation technologies to create new and exciting customer experiences as we shape a more sustainable, digital future. The Group’s purpose is grounded in empowering our people to innovate better ways to connect with customers, unlock new possibilities for future businesses to thrive and foster a sense of ownership and belonging to better serve our stakeholders, partners and communities.

Follow us on Twitter at www.twitter.com/SingtelNews. 
Find us on Facebook at https://www.facebook.com/singtel/

data.job_title: Director, Cyber Security Governance, Risk and Assurance
data.description:
An empowering career at Singtel begins with a Hello. Our purpose, to Empower Every Generation, connects people to the possibilities they need to excel. Every "hello" at Singtel opens doors to new initiatives, growth, and BIG possibilities that takes your career to new heights. So, when you say hello to us, you are really empowered to say…“Hello BIG Possibilities”.
  
  
**Be a Part of Something BIG!**
Reporting to the Group Chief Information Security Officer (GCISO), the Group Cyber Governance, Risk & Assurance Director is a senior Group-level leadership role accountable for setting, maintaining, and enforcing Singtel Group’s cyber security policies, standards, and compliance posture. The role owns the Group cyber policy framework, control standards, and assurance mechanisms that ensure cybersecurity risks are consistently identified, assessed, managed, and reported across all Operating Companies and Associates.
  
  
Working closely with OpCo risk and security leaders, the role defines Group-wide cyber governance requirements, including policies, minimum control standards, and risk acceptance principles. These policy guardrails establish clear expectations for due care, regulatory compliance, and “secure-by-default” outcomes, while allowing measured flexibility for local operating contexts.
  
  
As a policy authority and trusted governance advisor, the Group GRC Director provides independent oversight and challenge on major initiatives and material risk decisions. The role ensures that cybersecurity risks are formally assessed against Group policies and standards, that policy exceptions are governed through defined approval processes, and that residual risks are transparently escalated and accepted at the appropriate level.
  
  
This position requires strong risk judgement, regulatory insight, and executive influence, with the ability to translate complex technical and cyber risks into clear policy positions, compliance outcomes, and Board-level risk narratives. The role bridges business, technology, and security by embedding disciplined policy governance, compliance assurance, and accountability into the Group’s operating model.
  
  
**Make an Impact by**
**Governance, Policy & Standards**

- Act as the Group owner and governing authority for Singtel Group Cyber Security Policies, Standards, and Control Libraries, ensuring consistent application, resilience, and enforceability across all OpCos and Associates.
- Establish, maintain, and evolve Group-wide minimum cybersecurity requirements, including approval of material policy updates, standards enhancements, and control baselines.
- Continuously assess Singtel Group Cyber Security Policies and Standards against industry best practices and regulatory expectations (e.g. ISO/IEC, NIST, CIS Controls, GSMA), ensuring safeguards remain effective against the evolving threat landscape.
- Own and govern the policy exception and risk acceptance process, including escalation thresholds, decision authorities, and documentation of residual risk.
- Drive security culture and controls adoption through structured stakeholder engagement, training, and enablement across the Group.

**Controls Risk Framework & Risk Visibility**

- Define and own the Group Cybersecurity Controls and Risk Framework, including methodologies to assess inherent risk, control effectiveness, control maturity, and residual risk across cyber domains.
- Develop and maintain controls risk metrics, KRIs, and gap indicators to provide consistent, risk-based visibility of cybersecurity posture across Singtel Group.
- Translate controls and compliance outcomes into executive- and Board-level insights, supporting prioritisation, remediation planning, and informed risk decisions.

**Compliance Assurance, Automation & Continuous Monitoring**

- Own the Group Compliance Assurance Framework, defining how compliance with Group Cyber Security Policies and Standards is assessed, validated, and reported across OpCos and Associates.
- Lead the transition from point-in-time assessments to continuous controls assurance, leveraging technology to provide near-real-time visibility of controls effectiveness and cyber resilience.
- Establish and execute a Group-wide digital GRC platform strategy as the single source of truth for governance, risk management, compliance, exceptions, and audit evidence.
- Drive the adoption of automation and agentic AI to scale compliance assessments, evidence collection, and controls monitoring, while maintaining auditability, traceability, and regulator confidence.

**Business, Regulatory & Cyber Leadership**

- Serve as the Group authority on cyber governance and risk, providing trusted advisory and challenge to senior management and Boards on cybersecurity risk posture and material exposures.
- Maintain awareness of emerging cyber threats, technologies (e.g. AI, quantum), and regulatory developments, translating these into risk-aligned governance and control enhancements.
- Act as a key interface with auditors, and external assessors on matters related to cyber governance, compliance, and assurance.
- Provide thought leadership and effective stakeholder engagement, balancing business enablement with risk discipline and regulatory expectations across the Group.

**Budget & Resource Stewardship**

- Assist in planning and managing budgets for GRC programs.
- Recommend resource allocation strategies to optimize cost, scale, and talent effectiveness.

**Stakeholder**
**Engagement & Influence**

- Lead and contribute to cross-functional governance
- Collaborate with GRC leads across OpCos to align on Group cyber governance requirements, policy interpretation, risk assessment approaches, and compliance expectations, including the treatment of emerging technologies and new risk paradigms.
- Engage internal stakeholders across business, technology, and risk functions to ensure cybersecurity governance, risk posture, and compliance priorities are aligned with enterprise objectives and risk appetite.
- Drive Group-wide alignment and awareness of cyber risk and compliance priorities, promoting consistent understanding of policies, standards, and risk responsibilities across all Operating Companies and Associates.

**Skills For Success**

- Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, Cybersecurity, or a related field.
- At least 10 years of experience in cybersecurity, technology risk, or GRC roles, with demonstrated experience operating in Group-level, regional, or multi-entity environments, overseeing policy, risk, and compliance across complex organisations.
- Demonstrated experience leading and governing large-scale cybersecurity or technology risk initiatives, including setting Group standards, driving compliance programmes, managing material risk issues, and engaging senior executives and Boards.
- Deep expertise in cybersecurity governance, risk management, and control frameworks, including NIST CSF, ISO/IEC 27001/27002, CIS Controls, and risk-based control models, with the ability to set, interpret, and enforce Group-wide policy and control standards.
- Strong understanding of telco, cloud, and enterprise IT operating environments, including cloud platforms, identity and access management, data protection, and network security, to enable informed risk-based policy decisions and governance oversight of emerging technologies (e.g. AI and quantum).
- Proven ability to translate complex technical and cyber risks into clear governance positions, executive briefings, and Board-level risk narratives that support informed decision-making and risk acceptance.
- Professional certifications preferred: CISSP, CISM, CRISC, CCSP, or equivalent credentials supporting senior leadership in governance, risk, and compliance.
- Demonstrated awareness of emerging cyber threats, regulatory developments, and evolving risk paradigms, with the ability to anticipate impacts to Group policy, controls, and assurance approaches.

**Leadership And Collaboration**

- Exceptional leadership, collaboration, and team management skills.
- Ability to work effectively with diverse stakeholders, including subsidiaries in a conglomerate or multi-subsidiary environment.
- Strong analytical, decision-making, and problem-solving skills, especially in dynamic and high-pressure situations.

**Rewards that Go Beyond**

- Flexible work arrangements
- Full suite of health and wellness benefits
- Ongoing training and development programs
- Internal mobility opportunities

**Are you ready to say hello to BIG Possibilities?**
**Take the leap with Singtel to unlock new opportunities and accelerate your growth. Apply now and start your empowering career!**
**We are committed to a safe and healthy environment for our employees & customers and will require all prospective employees to be fully vaccinated.**

data.description:
**Be A Part of Something BIG!**
This
**Engineer, Cloud Infrastucture (Virtualization/ Windows)**
will be responsible for the Operations of our virtualization and container infrastructure in our customized OnPremise Private Cloud. The successful candidates will help maintain the hardware & virtualization environment in a high state of operational readiness, and in compliance with the necessary security measures.
  
  
He/she will help ensure that patching is carried out according to the defined schedule, and will work closely with the Change Management and the application teams to ensure that patches are applied on time. The candidate will help provide L1 and L2 support to applications for Windows & Linux OS related issues. as well has help coordinate any hardware RMA activity.
  
  
**Make An Impact By**

- Operations and maintenance of VMware, Nutanix, Openshift and HyperV and Compute hardware or software in on premises datacentres. Support Wintel & Linux related issues and requests and changes requested by application or infrstructure teams.
- Playing a major role in the operations of the virtualization infrastructure, including maintaining security tools, automation tools, outsourced vendor management if any
- Making sure that all Virtualization and OS infrastructure patches are up to date and infrastructure is current and resilient.
- Work with Change Management to propose and implement changes, especially for patching for ongoing quality, efficiency and resiliency requirements
- Ensure that operational SLA’s are met, and crafting meaningful reports and metrics to track progress of current infrastructure, including capacity planning and operational improvements
- Ensure operations are in compliance with security and best practices adopted by the industry
- Document and communicate processes and standards
- Ensure solution compliance and integration with enterprise architecture and Infosec policy for Infrastructure and Security systems by reviewing and advising on new and existing Information Technology projects
- Providing advice and guidance to management during planning phases to ensure that non-functional requirements are included in the overall specifications

**Skills For Success**

- Degree in computer science/ Information Technology, Industry certification
- 5-8 years of experience working and familiar with corporate infra operations in virtualization, Windows and Linux OS.
- Hands on troubleshooting skills to aid with the resolution of Redhat Openshift and HyperV and Compute hardware or software related issues.
- Experience in VMware environment troubleshooting and patching, including hardware firmware upgrades. VMware certification is a plus.
- Experience in Nutanix/ Red Hat Server administration and configuration
- Experience in OpenShift containers and virtualization is a plus.
- Possess Windows and Linux experience to be able to perform effective troubleshooting.
- Experience with Kubernetes infrastructure and Ansible scripting is a big plus.
- Familiar with security hygiene and best practices, compute/storage operations.
- Willing to work on shift basis (e.g. 12 hours shift)

**Your Career Growth Starts Here. Apply Now!**

data.description:
**Join the Growing Team at Singtel Financial Shared Services in Malaysia!**
Are you ready to embark in the exciting world of finance and technology?
**Singtel Financial Shared Services (FSS)**
is expanding its operations in Malaysia, and we're looking for passionate, driven individuals to join our dynamic team!
  
  
In Malaysia, Singtel Group operates through a fully owned subsidiary called
**Sudong Sdn. Bhd.**
**Why Singtel Finance Shared Services?**

- Grow Your Financial Digital Literacy: Enhance your expertise in the latest financial technologies and practices in a collaborative, fast-paced shared services environment.
- Impactful Work: Contribute to global operations while making a tangible impact on how businesses manage their financial operations.
- Career Growth: As we expand, you'll have plenty of opportunities to learn, grow, and advance in your career.

The
**Billing Manager**
is responsible for supporting the Director in overseeing billing operations within the Billing KLV Shared Services Hub and automation initiatives. This role involves leading and managing a team to perform User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for system changes impacting customer billing, ensuring billing accuracy, completeness, and timeliness.
  
  
The position also encompasses resource planning, process governance, continuous improvement initiatives, as well as documentation and operational user training.
  
  
**Make An Impact By**

- Leading, engaging, coaching, and developing a high-performing team to deliver quality outcomes.
- Overseeing software testing and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) of billing systems to ensure new builds, features, and workflows impacting billing are accurate, complete, and timely: (i) Acting as a billing advisor to support the business in launching new products and marketing initiatives (ii) Coordinating with business units and IT to identify system changes and enhancements affecting billing. (iii) Leading the preparation and execution of UAT to ensure testing is completed in accordance with agreed timelines, supporting timely rollout of initiatives. (iv) Implementing strong preventive and detective controls over data processing. (v) Ensure initiatives are rolled out with no negative impact on operations nor adverse feedback from customers. (vi) Ensure proper documentation and training are conducted upon completion of project/testing.
- Planning and managing testing resources and external demands to optimise team effectiveness in delivering timely and high-quality outcomes.
- Ensuring robust testing processes by: (i) Implementing controls to prevent and detect anomalies, while ensuring compliance with policies and guidelines. (ii) Monitoring service level agreements (SLAs) and ensuring targets are met, with timely corrective and preventive actions to minimise customer impact. (iii) Maintaining proper process documentation. (iv) Addressing process issues and concerns promptly.
- Monitoring and reporting on team performance and SLA achievement.
- Continuously reviewing work practices, processes, and procedures to enhance customer experience and operational efficiency through simplification, digitisation, and automation.
- Driving automation initiatives by collaborating with IT and Digital teams, defining requirements, and leading UAT for implementation.
- Supporting Project testing for billing for new system replacement; managing project resources; and defects management & Reporting
- Performing any other duties as assigned by the Director.

**Skills For Success**

- Bachelor's Degree in Accounting/ IT/ Business/Computer Science or equivalent
- At least 10 years' experience in Billing, testing or projects.
- Possesses Business process analytical skills.
- Highly proficient in Microsoft Office, Excel and Powerpoint.
- Demonstrates strong leadership with a proactive approach and a collaborative, team oriented mindset.
- Equipped with solid analytical abilities, strong attention to detail, and a meticulous work style.
- Communicates effectively in both written and verbal forms.
- Exhibits critical thinking, embraces a growth mindset, and upholds strong personal accountability.

**Advantageous to Have!**

- Experience in managing Billing operations, projects, or UAT testing.
- Background in the Shared Services industry.
- Prior involvement in process and data analysis for improvement initiatives and implementation of enhancements.
- Familiarity with SAP billing modules.
- Knowledge of Power BI, Power Automate, and Thinkcell.

**Join us on our mission to continue defining the future of financial services. Apply Now!**

**Make An Impact By**

- Responsible for designing, building, and managing the enterprise data ecosystem and AI infrastructure together with cross functional teams.
- Drive overall solution architecture design, ensuring alignment with business goals and technology standards.
- Oversee the design and implementation of data systems to support business intelligence, analytics, and AI initiatives.
- Architect and manage on-premise/cloud platforms to enable AI workloads, integrations, and advanced data processing.
- Lead teams in design, implementation and delivery of enterprise solutions, fostering technical excellence and collaboration.
- Partner with internal stakeholders and external vendors to evaluate technologies, select solutions, and ensure successful project execution and delivery.

**Skills For Success**

- Degree in Computer Science, Engineering or related discipline.
- >8 years in systems software development and solution architecture.
- Experience in end-to-end integrated Data, IoT and AI pipelines, as well as MLOps, DataOps and its related technologies.
- Proficient in AI infra systems and automation.
- Knowledge in AI development, deployment and its data orchestration, processing and lifecycle.
- Proficient in data and AI infrastructure tuning and performance optimization including networking, storage and compute.
- Strong leadership and business acumen with excellent communication and presentation skills.
- Self-motivated problem solver and collaborative team player committed to continuous learning.

**Are you ready to say hello to BIG Possibilities?**
**Take the leap with Singtel to unlock new opportunities and accelerate your growth. Apply now and start your empowering career!**

data.description:
**Join the Growing Team at Singtel Financial Shared Services in Malaysia\*!**
Are you ready to embark in the exciting world of finance and technology?
**Singtel Financial Shared Services (FSS)**
is expanding its operations in Malaysia, and we're looking for passionate, driven individuals to join our dynamic team!
  
  

- In Malaysia, Singtel Group operates through a fully owned subsidiary called Sudong Sdn. Bhd.

We are seeking for The
**Head of CM BPO Operations (Senior Manager)**
to be based in Kuala Lumpur. This position directly reports to the CM Senior Director, leading a team of approximately 70 staff across Receipting, Collections/Chasers, and Fraud and Dispute Operations. This role oversees Supervisors and Team Leads covering Receipting, Collections, Fraud/Investigations, Quality, Workforce Planning, and Projects/Systems Analysis, with overall accountability for operational performance, compliance, and service delivery. The position also drives operational projects and system and process transformation initiatives, ensuring continuous improvement and alignment with broader business objectives.
  
  
**Make An Impact By**
**Operational Leadership**

- Lead daily operations for Receipting, Chasers, and Fraud/Investigations teams, ensuring SLA, KPI, and compliance performance.
- Manage team leads and supervisors; drive productivity, accuracy, and service quality across functions.
- Oversee fraud/AML investigations, ensuring timely escalation and collaboration with compliance and risk.
- Maintain controls and audit readiness for receipting and cash applications.

**People Management**

- Lead, coach, and develop a 70-headcount team with clear goals, training, and career pathways.
- Oversee workforce planning, scheduling, occupancy, and attrition management.
- Foster a culture of accountability, teamwork, and continuous improvement.

**Project & System Management**

- Act as operational project lead for system enhancements, automation, process re-engineering, and integration initiatives.
- Partner with IT, finance, and service delivery teams on new system rollouts (e.g., ERP, workflow, or collection platforms).
- Define business requirements, perform UAT, and manage change communications.
- Drive digitalisation and process automation (RPA, dashboards, AI-based monitoring) to reduce manual effort and improve control.
- Track and report project milestones, timelines, and benefits realisation.

**Performance & Analytics**

- Define and track operational KPIs: cash collection, DSO, SLA adherence, quality, TAT, audit scores.
- Analyse trends, prepare dashboards, and present performance insights to senior management.
- Identify process bottlenecks and initiate corrective action plans.

**Stakeholder Management**

- Serve as the single point of contact for internal stakeholders (Finance, Credit, Risk, Compliance, Product, and IT).
- Manage BPO/vendor relationships and contract adherence.
- Provide transparent reporting and updates on operations and project progress.

**Skills For Success**

- 7–10 years in BPO / Shared Services operations, preferably in finance operations, receipting, collections, or fraud.
- Minimum 2–3 years in a leadership role managing 50+ FTEs.
- Proven project management experience (system implementation, process transformation, or automation).
- Strong business understanding of order-to-cash, credit, and fraud control processes.
- Hands-on experience with ERP systems (SAP / Oracle / Workday, etc.) and workflow tools.
- Analytical mindset — strong Excel/BI skills; familiarity with project tools (e.g., MS Project, JIRA, Power BI).
- Excellent communication in English; able to present to senior stakeholders and cross-functional teams.
- Preferred: PMP, Lean Six Sigma, or similar certification.
- Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, or related field.

**Apply Now!**

data.description:
An empowering career at Singtel begins with a Hello. Our purpose, to Empower Every Generation, connects people to the possibilities they need to excel. Every "hello" at Singtel opens doors to new initiatives, growth, and BIG possibilities that takes your career to new heights. So, when you say hello to us, you are really empowered to say…“Hello BIG Possibilities”.
  
  
At Singtel, we believe in the strength of a vibrant, diverse and inclusive workforce where backgrounds, perspectives and life experiences of our people help us innovate and create strong connections with our customers. We strive to ensure all our people practices are non-discriminatory and provide a fair, performance-based work culture that is diverse, inclusive and collaborative.
  
  
Join us and experience what it’s like to be with an Employer of Choice\*. Together, let’s create a brighter digital future for all. \*Awarded at the HR Fest Awards 2020.
  
  
**Be A Part Of Something Big**
Singtel Group is seeking a strategic and technically proficient Associate Director to accelerate AI adoption across Singtel Group. This role is ideal for someone with around 8-10 years of experience in AI and/or data analytics. A successful candidate will translate the Group AI agenda into practical execution and adoption. The role will support Group AI strategy and Playbook implementation, shape the direction of AI for Employee initiative, and drive selected acceleration initiatives across partnerships, influencer activation, communications, and process improvement. The candidate will bring strong portfolio and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to prioritise high-value AI opportunities, size and track business impact, represent user needs, provide input to product roadmaps, and drive change management to achieve sustained adoption. The role requires a balance of strategic thinking and hands-on execution, ensuring AI initiatives are business-relevant, user-centric, and aligned with Group priorities.
  
  
**Make An Impact By**
**Support Group AI Strategy and Playbook Implementation**
Contribute to the implementation of the
**Group AI strategy and Playbook**
by engaging OpCos and business stakeholders.
  
  

- Engage OpCos on strategy implementation
- Translate playbook guidance into practical execution actions
- Gather feedback from business and OpCo stakeholders
- Help ensure Group AI strategy is grounded in real delivery needs and adoption challenges
- Support alignment between strategy, delivery, communications, and change management

**Shape AI for Employee Initiative**
Work with
**Group Digital Office**
and
**Singtel Singapore AIDA**
to shape the direction of
**AI for Employee Initiative**
, ensuring the portfolio is focused on high-value opportunities and designed for strong adoption. The role is not to build or develop solutions, but to provide portfolio leadership across prioritisation, value sizing, user experience, roadmap input, and change management.
  
  

- Programme manage AI for Employee Initiative

- Shape the portfolio by prioritising use cases based on business value and feasibility
- Leverage the Steering Committee to drive outcomes from the initiative

- Represent employee and business user needs to improve user experience, work with Group Digital Office and STSG AIDA to shape the product roadmap of SingtelGPT

- Gather user feedback and translate insights into product roadmap input
- Support change management to drive awareness, readiness, adoption, and sustained usage
- Identify adoption or stakeholder blockers and work with relevant owners to resolve them

**Drive External Partnerships and Commercialisation**
Lead the External Partnerships portfolio, with a focus on converting ecosystem engagement into tangible business outcomes.
  
  

- Identify and shape AI partnership opportunities aligned to Group AI priorities
- Engage AI-native companies, start-ups, ecosystem players, and community partners
- Work with Singtel operating companies to translate partnerships into pilots, commercialisation opportunities, or capability-building initiatives
- Assess and prioritise partnership opportunities based on strategic fit, business value, and execution feasibility
- Work with internal stakeholders to connect external capabilities with relevant business use cases.

**Strengthen the AI Influencer Network**
Strengthen AI Influencer Network as a key mechanism to accelerate AI adoption across the organisation.
  
  

- Recruit and engage AI influencers across business units and functions.
- Equip influencers with necessary communication materials, toolkits, use cases, and success stories.
- Drive community engagement and peer-to-peer advocacy.
- Track the network’s contribution to awareness, adoption, and behavioural change.

**Support AI Communications**
Support group-level communications by providing stories and inputs.
  
  
Implement the weekly AI news digest as part of the broader Group AI communications agenda.
  
  

- Curate relevant external AI trends, use cases, and ecosystem developments
- Translate AI developments into concise, business-relevant insights for employees and stakeholders
- Maintain a consistent communications cadence

**Drive Process Improvement**
Identify opportunities to improve processes, for example, to enable AI initiatives to move faster from idea to implementation while maintaining appropriate governance.
  
  

- Identify process gaps, bottlenecks, and stakeholder pain points across AI acceleration initiatives, and co-design improvements
- Support refinement of operating models, ways of working, and decision-making forums.
- Improve stakeholder experience, clarity of ownership, and cycle time across key initiatives.
- Ensure processes are practical, scalable, user-centric, and aligned with Group AI priorities.

**Skills For Success**

- Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Engineering, Computer Science, Data / Analytics, or a related field.
- Practical experience deploying AI or GenAI solutions in an enterprise environment, from pilot to production, adoption, and measurable business impact.
- Good understanding of the end-to-end AI delivery lifecycle, including use-case prioritisation, business case development, delivery governance, adoption planning, benefits tracking, and post-launch feedback.
- Strong business acumen, with the ability to link AI initiatives to productivity, customer experience, revenue, cost efficiency, risk reduction, or operational improvement.
- Experience working across business, product, technology, data, risk, and governance teams to resolve deployment and adoption challenges.
- Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and simplify complex AI topics for business audiences.
- Strong change management mindset, with the ability to represent user needs, gather feedback, and drive sustained adoption of AI-enabled ways of working.

**Are you ready to say hello to BIG Possibilities?**
**Take the leap with Singtel to unlock new opportunities and accelerate your growth. Apply now and start your empowering career!**

company_object.name: Singtel
company_object.long_description:
Singtel is Asia's leading communications technology group, providing a portfolio of services from next-generation communication, 5G and technology services to infotainment to both consumers and businesses. The Group has presence in Asia, Australia and Africa and reaches over 740 million mobile customers in 21 countries. Its infrastructure and technology services for businesses span 21 countries, with more than 428 direct points of presence in 362 cities.  

For consumers, Singtel delivers a complete and integrated suite of services, including mobile, broadband and TV. For businesses, Singtel offers a complementary array of workforce mobility solutions, data hosting, cloud, network infrastructure, analytics and cyber security capabilities. 

Singtel is dedicated to continuous innovation, harnessing next-generation technologies to create new and exciting customer experiences as we shape a more sustainable, digital future. The Group’s purpose is grounded in empowering our people to innovate better ways to connect with customers, unlock new possibilities for future businesses to thrive and foster a sense of ownership and belonging to better serve our stakeholders, partners and communities.

Follow us on Twitter at www.twitter.com/SingtelNews. 
Find us on Facebook at https://www.facebook.com/singtel/

data.description:
**Position: Accounts Payable Executive**
**Job Location: Kuala Lumpur**
**Job Grade: OS5**
Are you ready to grow your career at the intersection of finance and technology? We are expanding our Finance Shared Services operations and are seeking passionate, driven individuals to join our dynamic team.
  
  
**Why Join Our Finance Shared Services Team?**
**Grow Your Financial Digital Literacy:**
Enhance your knowledge of the latest financial technologies and best practices within a collaborative, fast-paced shared services environment.
  
  
**Meaningful Work:**
Contribute to high-impact financial operations that support efficient business performance.
  
  
**Career Advancement Opportunities:**
As we continue to grow, you’ll have ample opportunities to learn, develop, and progress in your career.
  
  
**The Accounts Payable Executive**
reports to the Accounts Payable Manager and works closely with offshore AP teams, internal users, and external vendors. This role covers a broad range of AP functions and is responsible for day-to-day activities related to PO and non-PO invoice processing.
  
  
**Make An Impact By**
**Invoice Processing**

- Process PO invoices accurately in the designated finance system within agreed SLAs, including triaging and tracking invoices with PO or goods-receipt issues
- Process non-PO invoices within SLAs, identifying and following up on account assignment or funding-related issues
- Review non-AP submitted non-PO invoices to ensure accuracy and completeness of postings

**Vendor & Stakeholder Support**

- Manage inquiries from internal stakeholders and vendors in a professional and timely manner
- Generate vendor line-item reports to support account reconciliation activities
- Coordinate with the offshore AP Manager for escalations or issues requiring further resolution.

**Skills For Success**

- Diploma in Accountancy (minimum requirement)
- At least 3 years of experience in an Accounts Payable function
- Good accounting knowledge with experience using SAP or similar ERP/finance systems, supported by strong analytical skills
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office, especially Excel, with the ability to work efficiently and accurately
- Strong verbal and written communication skills, effective interpersonal abilities, and sound critical‑thinking capability
- Strong time‑management skills with a task‑oriented approach
- Meticulous, efficient, and capable of managing high transaction volumes with accuracy and consistency
- Experience with e‑invoice portals and participation in AP‑related projects, including UAT, is highly advantageous
- Familiarity with SAP S/4HANA and procurement/e‑invoicing platforms is an added advantage

**Join Us!**
**Become part of a team that is shaping the future of financial shared services. Apply now and take the next step in your career!**

data.description:
At Singtel, we believe in the strength of a vibrant, diverse and inclusive workforce where backgrounds, perspectives and life experiences of our people help us innovate and create strong connections with our customers. We strive to ensure all our people practices are non-discriminatory and provide a fair, performance-based work culture that is diverse, inclusive and collaborative.
  
  
Join us and experience what it’s like to be with an Employer of Choice\*. Together, let’s create a brighter digital future for all. \*Awarded at the HR Fest Awards 2020.
  
  
**Make An Impact By**

- Design, plan and setup/expand new/existing Singtel International Leased Circuit (ILC) infrastructure and ensuring that design conforms to offered Service Level Agreement, facilitate ease of operation and maintenance and adhere to the security best practices/standards.
- Design, plan, implement, manage the life cycle and replacement of end-to-end and bilateral DWDM and ASON/OTN ILC infrastructure, Network Management Systems and Data Communication Network.
- Manage infrastructure capacity to ensure that the performance meets the Service Level Agreements offered to customers.
- Manage contractors and internal stakeholders to perform roles such as site survey, installations, testing and commissioning, service activations and ensure milestones are tracked and delivered within a requested date of readiness.
- Enable the day 2 support structure by Operations team after implementation.
- Proactively lead the continuous network optimization initiatives including the Point of Presence (POP) and services migration exercise.
- Develop and conduct testing to support new features on Singtel ILC services.
- Upgrade and enhance the performance monitoring, service management and support system of the infrastructure.
- Work with Operation team on the commissioning, implementation of changes, decommissioning, and provisioning of service.
- Ensure maintenance, upgrades and new implementations are executed with minimal impact on business and customer operations.
- Act as Tier 3 support for resolving operational incidents and problems.
- Manage equipment/system vendors, purchase equipment through tendering process and administer supplier's contract.
- Responsible for the inventory record of ILC infrastructure in Digital Inventory (DI) system including the bilateral fibre connectivity with partners.
- Enhance the features and usability of the DI to support ILC services and how the circuit assignment can be optimized/automated through DI by working with the portal development team and relevant stakeholders.
- Keep abreast of new technologies and regional/global ILC market competitive landscape to improve the infrastructure and services that are offered to customers.
- Evaluate new products and technologies for new services rollout.
- Technical subject matter expert for the service and act as a consultant to the pre-sales team.
- Analyse, identify and drive the implementation on areas where automation can be applied to improve efficiency, reduce costs, or enhance quality of services.

**Skills For Success**

- Degree in Telecommunications/ Electrical/ Electronic or equivalent qualifications with at least 3 years of working experience.
- Good technical knowledge and relevant skillset to handle Layer 0 on Dark fibre, L1 DWDM, ASON, OTN, SDH, L2 Ethernet and L3 IP Network technology and platforms.
- Experience and exposure to industry leading DWDM, OTN and L2 platforms such as Ciena, Nokia/ Infinera, Ribbon, Huawei, etc.
- Good working experience and knowledge on the following will be added advantage:
- AAA (Authentication, Authorization and Accounting) related services, in particular RADIUS, TACACS, LDAP, 2FA, IPSec, SSL, etc.
- Software programming and integration, in particular Python, JSON, XML, REST, etc.
- Familiarity with enterprise security standards such as CIS (Center for Internet Security) Control, SOC 2 (Security Organization Control Type 2), ISO 27001, etc.
- Proficiency in Microsoft Productivity Suites: O365 (Excel, Words, Power Point, Visio), Teams, Co-Pilot and Chat-GPT is preferred.
- To have the aptitude and attitude to continuously upgrade and refresh the knowledge in optical transmission technologies, IP networking and Artificial Intelligence.
- Good inter-personal, organizational, problem solving and teamwork skills.
- Pro-active nature, s trong verbal and written communication skills, independent, play a hands-on role, ability to coordinate process designs across multiple function groups and adapt in a challenging and fast-paced environment, confident in dealing with various external and internal stakeholders with a customer-focused attitude.
- Flexible, open mind and curious on new and/or unfamiliar areas to understand holistically the relationship to the services offered to customers.

**Are you ready to say hello to BIG Possibilities?**
**Take the leap with Singtel to unlock new opportunities and accelerate your growth. Apply now and start your empowering career!**

data.description:
**Be a part of something BIG!**
Say
**HELLO to BIG Possibilities with Singtel KL**
(Sudong MY Sdn Bhd)!
  
  
Singtel is Asia's leading communications technology group, our presence spans across 21 countries in Asia, Australia, and Africa. We thrive on innovation, digital transformation, and harnessing next-generation technology to create a more sustainable and digital future. Now, we are bringing this energy to Kuala Lumpur with the launch of our brand-new Shared Services Centre.
  
  
We are looking for a
**Finance Executive – Capital Management**
to support capital management activities, ensure compliance with the Fixed Asset Management Control Framework, and contribute to efficient and accurate financial operations.
  
  
**Make An Impact By**

- WIP Monitoring & Capitalisation
- Conduct regular reviews of Work in Progress (WIP) balances and prepare reports
- Collaborate with stakeholders to verify balances, confirm asset readiness, and ensure accurate project status
- Track required documentation to support timely capitalisation of completed assets
- Prepare WIP ageing analysis and highlight delays with supporting insights
- Capital Management & Policy Compliance
- Support compliance with capital management policies and internal control frameworks
- Review documentation for completeness, accuracy, and alignment with policies
- Provide guidance to stakeholders on capital management processes
- Support development and updating of training and reference materials
- Month-End Close & Reporting
- Support month-end close activities, including reconciliations and reporting
- Prepare and distribute capitalisation and WIP-related reports
- Assist in management reporting on capital expenditure and performance
- Maintain accurate documentation and audit trails
- Process Improvement & Controls
- Support implementation of process improvements and control enhancements
- Maintain documentation such as SOPs, RACI, and risk control matrices
- Participate in control testing and validation activities
- Identify opportunities to streamline processes and improve efficiency
- Finance Transformation & Systems Support
- Support ERP system initiatives (e.g., SAP S/4HANA or similar systems), including testing and documentation
- Participate in user acceptance testing (UAT) and process design activities
- Assist in developing system documentation and user guides
- Support transition of processes to new systems and platforms

**Skills For Success**

- Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience
- Approximately 3–5 years of experience in financial accounting or related roles
- Professional accounting qualification (e.g., ACCA, CPA, CIMA) completed or in progress is an advantage
- Experience in capital accounting, fixed assets, or project accounting is advantageous
- Exposure to large or complex organisations or industries is a plus
- Strong foundation in accounting principles and financial reporting
- Ability to collaborate with teams and contribute to process or system improvements
- Familiarity with ERP systems (e.g., SAP S/4HANA or similar platforms)
- Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and provide insights
- Ability to support system enhancements and process improvements

**Your Career Growth Starts Here. Apply Now!**

data.job_title: Director, Cyber Security Governance, Risk and Assurance
data.description:
An empowering career at Singtel begins with a Hello. Our purpose, to Empower Every Generation, connects people to the possibilities they need to excel. Every "hello" at Singtel opens doors to new initiatives, growth, and BIG possibilities that takes your career to new heights. So, when you say hello to us, you are really empowered to say…“Hello BIG Possibilities”.
  
  
**Be a Part of Something BIG!**
Reporting to the Group Chief Information Security Officer (GCISO), the Group Cyber Governance, Risk & Assurance Director is a senior Group-level leadership role accountable for setting, maintaining, and enforcing Singtel Group’s cyber security policies, standards, and compliance posture. The role owns the Group cyber policy framework, control standards, and assurance mechanisms that ensure cybersecurity risks are consistently identified, assessed, managed, and reported across all Operating Companies and Associates.
  
  
Working closely with OpCo risk and security leaders, the role defines Group-wide cyber governance requirements, including policies, minimum control standards, and risk acceptance principles. These policy guardrails establish clear expectations for due care, regulatory compliance, and “secure-by-default” outcomes, while allowing measured flexibility for local operating contexts.
  
  
As a policy authority and trusted governance advisor, the Group GRC Director provides independent oversight and challenge on major initiatives and material risk decisions. The role ensures that cybersecurity risks are formally assessed against Group policies and standards, that policy exceptions are governed through defined approval processes, and that residual risks are transparently escalated and accepted at the appropriate level.
  
  
This position requires strong risk judgement, regulatory insight, and executive influence, with the ability to translate complex technical and cyber risks into clear policy positions, compliance outcomes, and Board-level risk narratives. The role bridges business, technology, and security by embedding disciplined policy governance, compliance assurance, and accountability into the Group’s operating model.
  
  
**Make an Impact by**
**Governance, Policy & Standards**

- Act as the Group owner and governing authority for Singtel Group Cyber Security Policies, Standards, and Control Libraries, ensuring consistent application, resilience, and enforceability across all OpCos and Associates.
- Establish, maintain, and evolve Group-wide minimum cybersecurity requirements, including approval of material policy updates, standards enhancements, and control baselines.
- Continuously assess Singtel Group Cyber Security Policies and Standards against industry best practices and regulatory expectations (e.g. ISO/IEC, NIST, CIS Controls, GSMA), ensuring safeguards remain effective against the evolving threat landscape.
- Own and govern the policy exception and risk acceptance process, including escalation thresholds, decision authorities, and documentation of residual risk.
- Drive security culture and controls adoption through structured stakeholder engagement, training, and enablement across the Group.

**Controls Risk Framework & Risk Visibility**

- Define and own the Group Cybersecurity Controls and Risk Framework, including methodologies to assess inherent risk, control effectiveness, control maturity, and residual risk across cyber domains.
- Develop and maintain controls risk metrics, KRIs, and gap indicators to provide consistent, risk-based visibility of cybersecurity posture across Singtel Group.
- Translate controls and compliance outcomes into executive- and Board-level insights, supporting prioritisation, remediation planning, and informed risk decisions.

**Compliance Assurance, Automation & Continuous Monitoring**

- Own the Group Compliance Assurance Framework, defining how compliance with Group Cyber Security Policies and Standards is assessed, validated, and reported across OpCos and Associates.
- Lead the transition from point-in-time assessments to continuous controls assurance, leveraging technology to provide near-real-time visibility of controls effectiveness and cyber resilience.
- Establish and execute a Group-wide digital GRC platform strategy as the single source of truth for governance, risk management, compliance, exceptions, and audit evidence.
- Drive the adoption of automation and agentic AI to scale compliance assessments, evidence collection, and controls monitoring, while maintaining auditability, traceability, and regulator confidence.

**Business, Regulatory & Cyber Leadership**

- Serve as the Group authority on cyber governance and risk, providing trusted advisory and challenge to senior management and Boards on cybersecurity risk posture and material exposures.
- Maintain awareness of emerging cyber threats, technologies (e.g. AI, quantum), and regulatory developments, translating these into risk-aligned governance and control enhancements.
- Act as a key interface with auditors, and external assessors on matters related to cyber governance, compliance, and assurance.
- Provide thought leadership and effective stakeholder engagement, balancing business enablement with risk discipline and regulatory expectations across the Group.

**Budget & Resource Stewardship**

- Assist in planning and managing budgets for GRC programs.
- Recommend resource allocation strategies to optimize cost, scale, and talent effectiveness.

**Stakeholder**
**Engagement & Influence**

- Lead and contribute to cross-functional governance
- Collaborate with GRC leads across OpCos to align on Group cyber governance requirements, policy interpretation, risk assessment approaches, and compliance expectations, including the treatment of emerging technologies and new risk paradigms.
- Engage internal stakeholders across business, technology, and risk functions to ensure cybersecurity governance, risk posture, and compliance priorities are aligned with enterprise objectives and risk appetite.
- Drive Group-wide alignment and awareness of cyber risk and compliance priorities, promoting consistent understanding of policies, standards, and risk responsibilities across all Operating Companies and Associates.

**Skills For Success**

- Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, Cybersecurity, or a related field.
- At least 10 years of experience in cybersecurity, technology risk, or GRC roles, with demonstrated experience operating in Group-level, regional, or multi-entity environments, overseeing policy, risk, and compliance across complex organisations.
- Demonstrated experience leading and governing large-scale cybersecurity or technology risk initiatives, including setting Group standards, driving compliance programmes, managing material risk issues, and engaging senior executives and Boards.
- Deep expertise in cybersecurity governance, risk management, and control frameworks, including NIST CSF, ISO/IEC 27001/27002, CIS Controls, and risk-based control models, with the ability to set, interpret, and enforce Group-wide policy and control standards.
- Strong understanding of telco, cloud, and enterprise IT operating environments, including cloud platforms, identity and access management, data protection, and network security, to enable informed risk-based policy decisions and governance oversight of emerging technologies (e.g. AI and quantum).
- Proven ability to translate complex technical and cyber risks into clear governance positions, executive briefings, and Board-level risk narratives that support informed decision-making and risk acceptance.
- Professional certifications preferred: CISSP, CISM, CRISC, CCSP, or equivalent credentials supporting senior leadership in governance, risk, and compliance.
- Demonstrated awareness of emerging cyber threats, regulatory developments, and evolving risk paradigms, with the ability to anticipate impacts to Group policy, controls, and assurance approaches.

**Leadership And Collaboration**

- Exceptional leadership, collaboration, and team management skills.
- Ability to work effectively with diverse stakeholders, including subsidiaries in a conglomerate or multi-subsidiary environment.
- Strong analytical, decision-making, and problem-solving skills, especially in dynamic and high-pressure situations.

**Rewards that Go Beyond**

- Flexible work arrangements
- Full suite of health and wellness benefits
- Ongoing training and development programs
- Internal mobility opportunities

**Are you ready to say hello to BIG Possibilities?**
**Take the leap with Singtel to unlock new opportunities and accelerate your growth. Apply now and start your empowering career!**
**We are committed to a safe and healthy environment for our employees & customers and will require all prospective employees to be fully vaccinated.**
